Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, indicating possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury). HbA1c is low, but current glucose is normal. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and monitoring of renal function and proteinuria.
